4 Introduction
Entanglement plays a role in quantum computing. Measuring some qubits makes others reach the desired position.
Consider searching for an object in an unordered array. A classical algorithm examines entries one by one until the object is found or the end of the array is reached. In the worst-case scenario, it looks at n entries.
A quantum computer can look at all entries simultaneously using superposition, resulting in fantastic speedup. Grover's algorithm finds the object in n queries to the array.
Shor's algorithm for factoring numbers uses superposition and number theory to look at many possible factors simultaneously.
Programming Languages
Algorithms must develop into concrete software. The bridge is programming. Researchers have started designing quantum programming languages.
Theoretical Computer Science
The goal of theoretical computer science is to formalize what engineers have done, and what they cannot do. Theoretical models of computation are described and classified.

4 Introduction
Entanglement plays a role in quantum computing. Measuring some qubits makes others reach the desired position.
Consider searching for an object in an unordered array. A classical algorithm examines entries one by one until the object is found or the end of the array is reached. In the worst-case scenario, it looks at n entries.
A quantum computer can look at all entries simultaneously using superposition, resulting in fantastic speedup. Grover's algorithm finds the object in n queries to the array.
Shor's algorithm for factoring numbers uses superposition and number theory to look at many possible factors simultaneously.
Programming Languages
Algorithms must develop into concrete software. The bridge is programming. Researchers have started designing quantum programming languages.
Theoretical Computer Science
The goal of theoretical computer science is to formalize what engineers have done, and what they cannot do. Theoretical models of computation are described and classified.

4 Introduction
Entanglement plays a role in quantum computing. Measuring some qubits makes others reach the desired position.
Consider searching for an object in an unordered array. A classical algorithm examines entries one by one until the object is found or the end of the array is reached. In the worst-case scenario, it looks at n entries.
A quantum computer can look at all entries simultaneously using superposition, resulting in fantastic speedup. Grover's algorithm finds the object in n queries to the array.
Shor's algorithm for factoring numbers uses superposition and number theory to look at many possible factors simultaneously.
Programming Languages
Algorithms must develop into concrete software. The bridge is programming. Researchers have started designing quantum programming languages.
Theoretical Computer Science
The goal of theoretical computer science is to formalize what engineers have done, and what they cannot do. Theoretical models of computation are described and classified.

The Implications of the Quantum World on Computer Science

From Certainty to Uncertainty
Heisenberg's uncertainty principle informs us that there are inherent limitations to knowledge about a physical system.

Architecture
Superposition generalizes the notion of bit to qubit. Many qubits together form quantum registers, enabling simultaneous multiple states.

Quantum gates manipulate qubits and follow quantum operations; reversible gates are necessary.

Algorithms
Quantum algorithms employ superposition for massive parallelism. Care is needed when measuring the computer in a superposition state.

4 Introduction
Entanglement plays a role in quantum computing. Measuring some qubits makes others reach the desired position.
Consider searching for an object in an unordered array. A classical algorithm examines entries one by one until the object is found or the end of the array is reached. In the worst-case scenario, it looks at n entries.
A quantum computer can look at all entries simultaneously using superposition, resulting in fantastic speedup. Grover's algorithm finds the object in n queries to the array.
Shor's algorithm for factoring numbers uses superposition and number theory to look at many possible factors simultaneously.
Programming Languages
Algorithms must develop into concrete software. The bridge is programming. Researchers have started designing quantum programming languages.
Theoretical Computer Science
The goal of theoretical computer science is to formalize what engineers have done, and what they cannot do. Theoretical models of computation are described and classified.
